name: 1Hosts
description: Advanced DNS filter/blocklists for privacy, security, and clean browsing.
readme_summary: 1Hosts provides curated DNS blocklists designed to enhance privacy, security, and user experience by filtering out ads, trackers, and malware. It offers different variants and formats compatible with various DNS filtering software and operating systems.
key_features: Support for various clients and formats (AdAway, Pi-hole, dnscrypt-proxy, uBlock Origin, AdGuardHome, dnsmasq, Unbound, Windows hosts file, Little Snitch)
Focus on privacy, security, and ad/tracker/malware blocking
primary_use_case: To provide users with comprehensive DNS blocklists that can be easily integrated into their existing network or device configurations to block unwanted content like ads, trackers, and malware.
cover_image_prompt: A digital garden where vibrant, healthy plants represent a clean and secure browsing experience. Intertwined thorny vines and shadowy figures representing ads, trackers, and malware are being neatly pruned and removed by a pair of glowing, digital shears labeled '1Hosts'. The background shows a subtle network of interconnected nodes, with some nodes being blocked by a translucent digital barrier. The overall mood is one of protection and order, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clear iconography and a balanced color palette of greens, blues, and subtle reds for blocked elements.

name: linux-android
description: A script that sets up linux on your anroid device via termux
readme_summary: This repository provides shell scripts to install and run a full Linux desktop environment or a Home Assistant smart home server on an Android device using Termux. It allows repurposing old Android phones without requiring root access or a PC.
key_features: Install Linux desktop environments (XFCE4, LXQt, MATE, KDE Plasma) on Android via Termux
Set up Home Assistant for smart home control
Supports GPU acceleration on Qualcomm Snapdragon chips
Provides scripts to start and stop the Linux environment
primary_use_case: Repurposing old Android phones into Linux desktops or smart home servers.
cover_image_prompt: A split image: on one side, an old, dusty Android phone is shown with a glowing, intricate circuit board pattern emerging from its screen, symbolizing the transformation. On the other side, the phone is depicted as a sleek, modern desktop computer monitor displaying a vibrant Linux desktop environment with icons for Firefox and Wine. Subtle digital particles flow from the phone to the monitor, representing the data transfer and repurposing. The background is a clean, minimalist workspace. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, optimistic color palette, clearly showing the phone's rebirth into a powerful computing device.

name: Open-LLM-VTuber
description: Talk to any LLM with hands-free voice interaction, voice interruption, and Live2D taking face running locally across platforms
readme_summary: Open-LLM-VTuber is a Python-based project enabling hands-free, voice-interactive AI companions with Live2D avatars. It supports real-time conversations, voice interruption, and can run entirely offline on local machines across different platforms.
primary_use_case: To create a personal, interactive AI companion with a visual avatar that can be controlled and conversed with using natural voice commands, running locally for privacy and accessibility.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized 3D character with a Live2D avatar appearance, sitting at a desk in a cozy room. The character is actively listening, indicated by a subtle glowing aura around its head, and speaking, with sound waves emanating from its mouth. In front of the character is a holographic interface displaying a real-time transcription of a conversation and a visualization of an LLM processing text. Small, subtle icons representing voice input and Live2D animation are visible on the interface. The overall mood is warm and inviting, with soft lighting. The style should be a clean, modern 3D illustration with a focus on clarity and approachability, subtly incorporating the 'Open-LLM-VTuber' name on a small monitor in the background.

name: babysitter
description: Babysitter enforces obedience on agentic workforces and enables them to manage extremely complex tasks and workflows through deterministic, hallucination-free self-orchestration
readme_summary: Babysitter is a JavaScript library designed to enforce strict obedience and deterministic execution within AI agent workforces. It enables the management of complex tasks and workflows by ensuring agents follow predefined steps, pass quality gates, and undergo human approval when necessary, all while maintaining an immutable record of decisions.
key_features: Ensures quality gates pass before progression
Records every decision in an immutable journal
Supports multiple AI coding harnesses (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor, Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Pi, Oh-My-Pi, OpenCode)
Includes an internal harness for programmatic execution without external AI agents
primary_use_case: To provide a framework for building and managing reliable, auditable, and controlled AI agent workflows, ensuring they execute tasks precisely as defined and preventing unintended or erroneous actions.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous clockmaker carefully assembling an intricate automaton. Gears and springs representing AI agents are precisely guided by the clockmaker's tools, ensuring each component clicks into place perfectly. Input scrolls of instructions are fed into a slot, and the automaton emerges fully functional, emitting a soft, steady glow. Small, subtle UI elements showing workflow steps and quality checks are integrated into the clockmaker's workbench. The scene is set in a well-lit, organized workshop with a clean, slightly futuristic aesthetic. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on precision and order, using a palette of warm brass, polished wood, and cool blue digital accents.

name: hermes-webui
description: Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!
readme_summary: Hermes WebUI provides a lightweight, dark-themed web interface for the Hermes Agent, offering full parity with its CLI experience. It allows users to interact with the autonomous agent through a browser, managing sessions, workspaces, and configurations with a user-friendly three-panel layout.
key_features: Circular context ring for token usage
primary_use_case: To provide a convenient and accessible web-based interface for interacting with the Hermes Agent, mirroring its full command-line functionality.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian meticulously organizing a vast, glowing library where each book represents a learned skill or memory of an AI agent. The librarian is using a sleek, modern tablet (representing the web UI) to access and manage these books, with digital particles flowing from the tablet to the shelves. In the background, subtle code snippets and data streams are visible, hinting at the underlying technology. The overall mood is one of organized intelligence and accessible power, with a dark, sophisticated color palette. The composition is clean and focused, highlighting the librarian's interaction with the library's knowledge. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a touch of futuristic design.
